prose:
Frontier-orbitals task on pyridine, computed with DFT at tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI on by default) in PySCF (RKS, closed-shell), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz (11 atoms), used as a fixed single-point geometry (not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged (10 cycles). Engine integrity verdict: trustworthy=true (status ok).

Headline result: HOMO-LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV. HOMO (index 20) = -7.2525992 eV; LUMO (index 21) = -1.112786 eV; HOMO-1 = -7.4731156 eV; HOMO-2 = -8.0425195 eV; LUMO+1 = -0.67231536 eV; LUMO+2 = 1.1781069 eV. Total electronic energy = -248.3782 hartree (-6758.7152 eV). Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 7.2525992 eV, vertical EA = 1.112786 eV, electronegativity = 4.1826926 eV, chemical hardness = 3.0699066 eV, chemical softness = 0.32574281 eV^-1, electrophilicity index = 2.8494218 eV.

Caveats: this gap is a Koopmans-style orbital-energy gap, not an optical excitation energy; density fitting (RI) is an approximation (chemkit default); energy zeros are method-specific and not cross-method comparable. The result JSON's warnings array was empty (no warnings reported). Command: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard .../pyridine.xyz. No experimental comparison offered (none requested).
